APIs and AI: It's complicated

APIs and AI: It's complicated Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(4) (How) does AI affect APIs?



APIs and AI: It's complicated Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(5) AI Success Stories

  • Necessary but not sufficient
    1. Building AI solutions well is necessary
    2. Establishing connections to capabilities is necessary
    3. Managing the foundations well is necessary


APIs and AI: It's complicated Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(6) The API Landscape Lifecycle for AI

  • Not about single APIs → It's about the API landscape
  • Lifecycle of evolving towards AI readiness
    1. Step #1: More APIs [Step #1: More APIs (1)]
    2. Step #2: Better Described APIs [Step #2: Better Described APIs (1)]
    3. Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery [Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery (1)]
    4. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs (1)]


Step #1: More APIs

Outline (Step #1: More APIs)

  1. APIs and AI: It's complicated [3]
  2. Step #1: More APIs [7]
  3. Step #2: Better Described APIs [5]
  4. Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery [7]
  5. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [4]
  6. Conclusions [2]
Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(8) Why More APIs Matter



Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(9) More APIs & Tools



Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(10) Model Context Protocol (MCP)

  • MCP is the current standard protocol for LLM consumers
  • The initial focus was on additional and extended context
    • Additional context by providing access to the environment
    • Extended context by allowing MCP servers to externally manage context
  • Many mapping tools generate MCP servers from OpenAPI descriptions
  • What to expose and how to design it is out of scope


Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(11) OpenClaw: The New AI Kid on the Block

  • A persistent and self-improving AI agent
  • Multi-channel and multi-agent
  • API-native by design


Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(12) MCP all the APIs

  1. Generate MCP from OpenAPI
  2. Publish/Install MCP servers for all APIs
  3. Consumers can access all MCP tools


Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(13) MCP all the APIs: Easy but Limited

  • Pros:
    • Zero development effort, many tools auto-generate MCP
    • All APIs easily available
    • Great for quick and focused experimentation
  • Cons:
    • Context window overflow happens quickly
    • AI struggles with understanding workflows across endpoints
    • Risk of misinterpreting MCP problems as general AI problems
  • Good as a starting point but lacks sophistication and scalability


Step #1: More APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(14) More Tools ⇏ Better Tools Landscape

  • LLMs can now access many tools
  • But they cannot understand many of them well
  • Poor descriptions → poor tool selection → poor results
  • Next: Step #2: Better Described APIs [Step #2: Better Described APIs (1)]


Step #2: Better Described APIs

Outline (Step #2: Better Described APIs)

  1. APIs and AI: It's complicated [3]
  2. Step #1: More APIs [7]
  3. Step #2: Better Described APIs [5]
  4. Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery [7]
  5. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [4]
  6. Conclusions [2]
Step #2: Better Described APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(16) Better Described APIs



Step #2: Better Described APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(17) Do Your DX Homework

  • Most API descriptions need quality improvements anyway
  • Better descriptions benefit everyone, not just AI agents
    • Complete and meaningful operation summaries
    • Comprehensive examples for all operations
    • Clear error handling
    • Consistent terminology and naming
  • Good DX is the foundation for good AX


Step #2: Better Described APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(18) Now There's AX Homework, Too

  • AI agents need information developers don't typically require:
    • Everything that developers usually just know
    • Treat every API like an external one for developers with no context
  • OpenAPI Initiative (OAI) might add AX-specific fields
  • Until then, leverage description fields strategically


Step #2: Better Described APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(19) AI Readiness Score for APIs



Step #2: Better Described APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(20) Better Descriptions ⇏ Scalable Tool Use

  • Better AX reduces confusion and misuse
  • But well-described APIs still can overload context
  • The problem shifts from understanding APIs to finding the right ones
  • Next: Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery [Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery (1)]


Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery

Outline (Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery)

  1. APIs and AI: It's complicated [3]
  2. Step #1: More APIs [7]
  3. Step #2: Better Described APIs [5]
  4. Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery [7]
  5. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [4]
  6. Conclusions [2]
Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(22) Dynamic API Discovery



Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(23) FastMCP Tool Search



Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(24) One API ≠ One Tool

  • APIs can be large and multifunctional
  • Tools should be task-specific
  • AI rarely needs an entire API


Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(25) GitHub API

  • GitHub API [https://github.com/github/rest-api-description]
    • 11MB
    • 320k lines
    • 738 paths
    • 1114 operations
    • 259 webhooks
  • GitHub MCP Server [https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server]
    • 19 toolsets (+3 remote)
    • 70 tools overall


Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(26) API Microproducts

  • Traditional API products bundle endpoints into coarse offerings
  • With unknown use cases, it's better to decompose into fine-grained API microproducts
    • OpenAPI: Treat all operations on all endpoints as individual capabilities
    • AsyncAPI: Treat all messages on all channels as individual capabilities
    • GraphQL: Treat all queries and mutations as individual capabilities
  • Allows flexible combination of capabilities for any use case


Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(27) Semantic Search with RAG

  • All capabilities indexed in a vector database
  • Queries resolved via semantic search (embeddings & similarity)
  • Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) assembles relevant operations
  • Depends heavily on high-quality descriptions


Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(28) Dynamic Discovery ⇏ Ideal API Landscape

  • Discovery helps reduce tool overload
  • Agents still have to invent workflows from scratch
  • We need higher-level, business-aligned APIs
  • Next: Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs (1)]


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs

Outline (Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs)

  1. APIs and AI: It's complicated [3]
  2. Step #1: More APIs [7]
  3. Step #2: Better Described APIs [5]
  4. Step #3: Dynamic API Discovery [7]
  5. Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs [4]
  6. Conclusions [2]
Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(30) Workflow-Based APIs



Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(31) Why Workflows Matter

  • Many tasks follow predictable, repeatable sequences
  • Agents shouldn't re-discover the same steps
  • Workflow APIs reduce the complexity of planning tasks
  • Higher-level APIs = less context, less confusion, faster results


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(32) Workflow Advantages

  • Predictability
  • Auditability
  • Compliance
  • Business alignment
  • Reduced need for AI
  • Energy/cost efficiency


Step #4: Workflow-Based APIs Erik Wilde: Beyond MCP: Scaling AI Enablement for API Landscapes

(33) Workflow APIs ⇏ Safe API Use

  • Workflows help with AI productivity
  • Detecting/defining workflows is non-trivial
  • AI needs a safe space to work with APIs
  • Outlook: Baking security into the platform
